title: S6: Layer/Filter Architecture — performance + toggle granularity
description:
Design spike to determine the right architecture for feature visibility management.

## The Question
QGIS uses ~100 vector layers. MapLibre has a fundamentally different model (sources + style layers + filters). What's the right architecture for:
- Toggling individual team members on/off
- Toggling breadcrumbs vs current positions
- Toggling marker categories (clues, hazards, IPP/LKP, casualties)
- Toggling individual search areas / drawings
- Performance with 30+ devices, 8-hour mission, 30s tracking interval (~28K breadcrumb points)

## Options to evaluate
1. **QGIS-style: many MapLibre layers** (1 per device, 1 per drawing)
2. **Few sources, filter-based visibility** (GeoJSON properties + setFilter)
3. **Hybrid: source-per-category, filter-per-item** (tracking source, markers source, drawings source)

## Performance test
- [ ] Generate synthetic data: 30 devices × 8 hours × 30s interval = 28,800 breadcrumb points
- [ ] Render all breadcrumbs in MapLibre — measure FPS
- [ ] Toggle visibility of individual devices — measure response time
- [ ] Compare: many-layers vs few-sources+filters approach
- [ ] Test on a mid-range laptop (not just M4 Max)

## UX test
- [ ] Prototype a filter panel (show/hide by person, by category)
- [ ] Compare with QGIS layer tree UX — is the filter panel simpler for non-tech users?

## Reference
Current plugin layer management:
~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/controllers/layers_controller.py
~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/layers/manager.py
~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/layers/schema.py

## Decisions needed

- Autosave interval (60s?)
- Backup file location and naming
- Mission archive/export format

## Sub-beads: TBD after research

title: M10: Integration test — full mission flow E2E
description:
End-to-end Playwright test of a complete SAR mission.

## Deliverable
- Playwright E2E test that runs a full simulated mission:
  1. Open app → map loads
  2. Start mission with name
  3. Traccar positions arrive (mocked) → devices appear on map
  4. Place IPP/LKP marker
  5. Place clue marker
  6. Draw search area, assign to team
  7. Add range rings around IPP
  8. Add bearing line
  9. Toggle device visibility
  10. Measure distance between two points
  11. Pause mission → verify timer stops
  12. Resume mission → verify timer resumes
  13. Kill app → reopen → verify resume prompt
  14. Finish mission
  15. Verify all data persisted correctly

## Decisions (open)
- Mock Traccar or use real server? (recommendation: mock for CI, real for manual testing)
- How to simulate app crash in Playwright?

## Dependencies
- All milestones M1-M9 complete

title: M5: Mission UI — start/pause/resume/finish + timers
description:
Mission lifecycle controls and dual timer display.

## Deliverable
- Mission control panel: Start, Pause/Resume, Finish buttons (colour-coded, flashing when paused)
- Dual timers: Elapsed (total since start) + Active Search (excludes pauses)
- Mission name input on start
- Start offset: back-date mission start 0-48 hours (spinner, for delayed command post setup and testing)
- Finish confirmation dialog — data remains editable after finish
- Separate Finalize step to lock data read-only (reversible with admin)
- Auto-detect paused mission on restart → prompt Resume or Start Fresh
- Mission state persisted to SQLite

## Behaviour (from plugin investigation — cite: mission_controller.py, mission_lifecycle_controller.py)

### State Machine
IDLE → ACTIVE → PAUSED ↔ ACTIVE → FINISHED → IDLE
- No backwards from FINISHED. Must start a new mission.
- FINISHED auto-transitions to IDLE internally.

### Pause
- Active search timer stops. Elapsed timer keeps running.
- Tracking data still accepted (auto-refresh suppressed, manual refresh works)
- Markers and drawings CAN still be placed/edited
- UI: Pause button becomes Resume with flashing animation

### Finish
- Stops all timing, resets to IDLE
- Data remains EDITABLE — not read-only
- Dialog says: 'Keep all mission data editable. Use Finalize later to archive and lock.'
- Incoming tracking stops (is_active returns false)
- Saved pause state cleared

### Finalize (separate from Finish)
- Creates archive ZIP (GeoPackage snapshot + project + attachments)
- Marks mission read-only (all layers set read-only)
- Reversible with admin credentials (unlock feature)
- Only available after mission reaches IDLE

### Restart Recovery
- On startup: check for persisted pause state
- If found: show Resume/Start Fresh dialog (NOT auto-resume)
- Resume: restore mission name, timers, pause state
- Start Fresh: clear saved state

### Back-dated Start
- Range: 0-48 hours (changed from plugin's 5h to support testing scenarios)
- Breadcrumbs fetched from the back-dated time
- Use case: teams already moving before command post starts, or replaying test data

### Single Active Mission
- One active mission at a time
- Multiple can exist on disk
- Starting new mission while paused is blocked — must resume or finish first

## Dependencies
- M2 complete (panel sits alongside map)
- M3 complete (mission state persisted to SQLite)

## Spike reference
- ~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/controllers/mission_lifecycle_controller.py
- ~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/controllers/mission_controller.py
- Investigation output: /tmp/m5-lifecycle.md

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Readiness review: Research required = Medium-High. Implementation readiness = 3/5. Recommended action: research one contradiction first, then implement. Core contradiction to resolve: the bead says FINISHED auto-transitions to IDLE internally, but M3 persistence/archive flow currently treats finished/finalized missions as persistent mission states for archive/export. We need one canonical lifecycle model before coding. Open questions needing confirmation/research: 1) exact persisted mission statuses we want in v1 (, , , ,  as UI-only or persisted?), 2) whether Finalize is in M5 scope or deferred to a later bead, 3) what reversible

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Corrected readiness review: Research required = Medium-High. Implementation readiness = 3/5. Recommended action: resolve one lifecycle contradiction before implementation. Core contradiction: the bead says finished missions auto-transition to idle, but current M3 persistence/archive design treats finished and finalized as meaningful persisted states. Open questions needing confirmation: 1) exact persisted mission statuses wanted in v1: active, paused, finished, finalized, and whether idle is UI-only or persisted, 2) whether Finalize belongs in M5 or a later bead, 3) what reversible with admin means in a desktop app, 4) exact recovery behavior for Start Fresh, 5) whether finishing a mission stops all background tracking immediately or allows manual fetch/review only. Architectural note: keep the mission state machine in a dedicated domain or application module, not inside UI components or persistence adapters. Use this corrected comment as the authoritative M5 readiness note.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Research follow-up requested for M5. Please resolve these before implementation: 1) Canonical lifecycle model: should finished missions remain persisted as finished until explicitly finalized, or should the UI transition to idle while persistence still retains a finished state? We need one model that matches M3 archive behavior. 2) Finalize scope: is Finalize part of M5 or should M5 stop at start/pause/resume/finish and leave finalize/unlock to a later bead? 3) Persisted statuses: confirm the exact status set we want in v1 and whether idle is a persisted mission status or only a UI/app-shell state. 4) Finish behavior: after Finish, does all tracking stop immediately, or is manual review/fetch still allowed while data remains editable? 5) Pause behavior: does background polling continue while auto-refresh is suppressed, or does polling stop entirely until resume/manual refresh? 6) Start Fresh on restart: does it merely dismiss the paused mission prompt, create a new mission, or explicitly archive/clear anything from the paused mission? 7) Admin unlock: what does reversible with admin mean in a desktop app for v1: local passphrase, hidden operator action, role-gated setting, or defer entirely? 8) Timer/domain rules: confirm the exact formulas for elapsed vs active-search time, especially with back-dated starts and multiple pause/resume cycles. 9) UI recovery copy: lock the actual operator wording for Resume vs Start Fresh and for Finish vs Finalize confirmation. Use this research to rewrite the bead body so the lifecycle model is unambiguous before coding starts.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Completed a pre-M8 structural hardening pass on the map subsystem. Split the former use-map-controller hotspot into explicit lifecycle, overlay-sync, and marker-interaction hooks while keeping the public controller surface stable. Also added focused unit coverage for marker-map interaction helpers and kept the full verification matrix green so M8 drawing work can land on a cleaner boundary.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: M8 readiness review from implementation side. Before coding, please lock these decisions explicitly: 1) Magnetic declination for v1 — repo guidance currently recommends fixed -4.5° for Ireland (true -> magnetic subtract 4.5°, magnetic -> true add 4.5°). Confirm whether this is the final v1 rule. 2) LPB range ring distances — need the subject categories and the concrete 25/50/75/95% distances/source data that should be used. Please do not leave this to implementation-time inference. 3) Drawing tool activation UX — recommend a single toolbar with one active tool at a time; click tool to arm, Esc to cancel, and return to select mode on finish/cancel. Confirm or replace with the intended operator workflow. Architecture note for M8: drawing sync/interaction should be introduced as drawing-specific modules and should not turn use-map-overlays into the next orchestration hotspot.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text:
RESEARCH COMPLETE — all 3 open questions resolved (2026-04-09 by Forge)

## 1. Magnetic Declination — LOCKED: fixed -4.5° for v1

The plugin hardcodes -4.5° at bearing_tool.py:45 (MAGNETIC_DECLINATION_IRELAND = -4.5).
Current actual value for Kerry (Carrauntoohil area) is approximately -4° to -5° depending on exact location.
The plugin stores TRUE bearing and converts for display only.

Conversion rules:
- True → Magnetic: magnetic = true + (-4.5) = true - 4.5°
- Magnetic → True: true = magnetic - (-4.5) = magnetic + 4.5°
- Both normalize to 0-360°

Decision: keep fixed -4.5° for v1. Dynamic WMM is Phase 3. Add a note in settings that this is approximate.

## 2. LPB Distances — LOCKED: from Koester/NASAR (already in plugin)

Source: 'Lost Person Behavior' by Robert Koester, NASAR guidelines.
Defined in plugin at utils/lpb_statistics.py:18-82.

| Category | 25% | 50% | 75% | 95% |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Child (1-3) | 100m | 300m | 700m | 1900m |
| Child (4-6) | 200m | 500m | 1100m | 2400m |
| Child (7-12) | 500m | 1300m | 2500m | 3800m |
| Hiker | 800m | 2000m | 4000m | 8000m |
| Hunter | 1200m | 3000m | 5500m | 10000m |
| Elderly | 200m | 500m | 1200m | 2500m |
| Dementia | 100m | 300m | 800m | 2000m |
| Despondent | 200m | 500m | 1500m | 3000m |
| Autistic | 200m | 600m | 1200m | 2000m |

Ring colours: 25%=green, 50%=yellow, 75%=orange, 95%=red.

## 3. Tool Activation UX — LOCKED: one-active-at-a-time, matches plugin

- Single toolbar, one active tool at a time
- Click tool button to arm it
- ESC cancels current drawing
- Tools auto-deactivate after creating a feature (one-shot)
- Right-click finishes multi-point tools (line, polygon)
- Activating another tool deactivates the current one
- Default returns to pan/select mode after deactivation
- Active tool indicator shown in UI ('Active: Bearing', etc.)

All three questions are now locked. M8 implementation readiness: 5/5.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Implemented M8 end to end. Added drawing math/LPB data, typed drafts/builders, expanded drawing runtime/store, drawing-specific GeoJSON and overlay sync, drawing-specific map interactions, toolbar/dialog UI, and browser-harness support. Kept drawing overlay logic out of the generic map overlay hook so the post-M7 architecture stayed clean. Added unit coverage for drawing math/LPB/builders/geojson/runtime and Playwright coverage for create/edit/delete flows plus marker/drawing interaction isolation. Verification complete: npm run test, npm run lint, npm run build, npm run test:e2e, cargo test --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml all passed.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Follow-up headed browser validation completed after closure. Confirmed the map renders visually in a real browser session, tile requests succeeded, and no browser-console errors were present. Also found and fixed a genuine UI issue where the drawing toolbar could intercept basemap button clicks; re-verified basemap switching plus focused M2/M8 Playwright coverage after the layout hardening.

title: M6: Markers — IPP/LKP, clues, hazards, casualties
description:
Click-to-place markers with type-specific forms and persistence.

## Deliverable
- Click map to place marker
- Type selector: IPP/LKP (blue), Clue (orange), Hazard (red), Casualty (red star)
- Per-type forms:
  - IPP/LKP: name, description, subject category (Child, Hiker, Elderly, etc.)
  - Clue: name, type (Footprint, Clothing, Equipment, Sighting, Evidence), confidence, found_by
  - Hazard: name, type (Cliff, Water, Bog, Vegetation, Wildlife, Weather, Other)
  - Casualty: name, description
- Auto-populated Irish Grid reference from click coordinates
- Markers persisted to SQLite via MissionStore
- Edit and delete markers

## Decisions (open)
- Marker icon design (SVG? emoji? custom?)
- Form UI (modal? sidebar panel?)
- Marker label display on map

## Dependencies
- M2 complete (map for placing markers)
- M3 complete (persistence for storing markers)

## Spike reference
- spikes/S3-drawing-tools/ — marker placement prototype
- spikes/S2-irish-grid/ — coordinate conversion for grid reference display

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Readiness review: Research required = Medium. Implementation readiness = 4/5. Recommended action: implement after locking a few product/UI decisions. Locked enough to start: click-to-place marker flow, marker types, SQLite persistence via MissionStore, Irish Grid auto-population, edit/delete capability. Open questions needing confirmation/research: 1) marker presentation choice (custom SVG set is strongly preferable for clarity/accessibility, but should be explicitly locked), 2) editing UI location (sidebar vs modal; sidebar is likely better for repeated ops), 3) label display rule on map (always, selected-only, zoom-threshold, or toggle), 4) canonical field set per marker type because current persistence schema already supports richer casualty/hazard fields than the bead text lists, 5) exact subject category list for IPP/LKP if we want controlled values rather than free text. Main architectural note: keep marker forms/schema mapping separate from map interaction logic so M6 does not turn map-view into a placement/editing blob.

title: M2: Map — MapLibre + basemaps + coordinate display
description:
Map rendering with basemap switching and live coordinate bar.

## Deliverable
- MapLibre GL JS rendering in the Tauri window via raw useRef
- Basemap switcher with 4 sources (see locked decisions below)
- Selected basemap persisted to localStorage
- Coordinate bar showing WGS84 + Irish Grid (TM65) on mouse move
- Map centered on Kerry (51.97, -9.70), default zoom 12
- Service Worker tile caching for offline resilience

## Decisions (locked)
- Raw MapLibre via useRef — no react-map-gl (decided M1 research)
- proj4js for TM65 conversion using TOWGS84 params from S2 spike
- Service Worker for tile caching (Tauri filesystem for GeoPackage deferred)

### Basemap URLs (locked)
1. **OpenTopoMap (default):** `https://tile.opentopomap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png` — attribution: © OpenTopoMap contributors
2. **ESRI World Topo:** `https://services.arcgisonline.com/arcgis/rest/services/World_Topo_Map/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}` — attribution: © Esri — NOTE: tile order is {z}/{y}/{x}
3. **OpenStreetMap:** `https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png` — attribution: © OpenStreetMap contributors
4. **ESRI Satellite:** `https://services.arcgisonline.com/arcgis/rest/services/World_Imagery/MapServer/tile/{z}/{y}/{x}` — attribution: © Esri — NOTE: tile order is {z}/{y}/{x}

### Coordinate bar format (locked)
Match the existing QGIS plugin display conventions:
- WGS84: 6 decimal places with directional suffix — e.g. `52.274681°N, 9.530912°W`
- TM65 Irish Grid Reference: 5-digit format — e.g. `V 80245 84452`
- Display side by side: `52.274681°N, 9.530912°W  |  V 80245 84452`

### Offline resilience promise for v1 (locked)
- Service Worker caches tiles as viewed — previously viewed tiles available offline
- No pre-cache or bulk tile download in v1
- If user pans to an unvisited area while offline, tiles will be blank
- GeoPackage-based offline maps deferred until file arrives from Eamonn

## Dependencies
- M1 complete (scaffold running — ✅ done)

## Open items (non-blocking)
- GeoPackage offline tile loading deferred until KMRT_package.gpkg arrives

## Spike reference
- spikes/S1-osi-maps/ — basemap switcher, caching, coordinate bar (29 tests)
- spikes/S2-irish-grid/ — proj4js coordinate conversion (201 tests)

Added tests first for basemap catalogue and coordinate formatting, then verified the feature with unit, lint, build, Playwright, and full test:all runs.

title: S1: OSI Maps — Validate MapLibre + MapGenie WMTS + offline tiles
description:
Spike to determine if OSI Discovery Series 1:50,000 maps can be loaded in MapLibre GL JS and cached for offline use.

## Goal
Prove or disprove that we can replicate the exact same OSI maps the team uses in QGIS, both online and offline.

## Tasks
- [ ] Confirm MapGenie credentials/endpoint with Eamonn
- [ ] Load OSI WMTS tiles in a MapLibre prototype
- [ ] Test offline tile caching — does it work without network?
- [ ] Verify contour/terrain detail at operational zoom levels (10-16)
- [ ] Confirm licensing allows offline caching
- [ ] Compare quality vs QGIS rendering

## BLOCKING
If this fails, the project cannot proceed. OSI maps are non-negotiable for mountain rescue.

## Reference
spikes/S1-osi-maps/README.md

title: M10: Integration test — full mission flow E2E
description:
Epic: End-to-end integration testing of a complete SAR mission.

## Deliverable
- Playwright E2E test that runs a full simulated mission:
  1. Open app → map loads
  2. Start mission with name
  3. Traccar positions arrive → devices appear on map
  4. Place IPP/LKP marker
  5. Place clue marker
  6. Draw search area, assign to team
  7. Add range rings around IPP
  8. Add bearing line
  9. Toggle device visibility
  10. Measure distance between two points
  11. Pause mission → verify timer stops
  12. Resume mission → verify timer resumes
  13. Kill app → reopen → verify resume prompt
  14. Finish mission
  15. Verify all data persisted correctly

## Dependencies
- All previous milestones (M1-M9)

## Decisions needed
- Mock Traccar or use real server?
- How to simulate app crash in Playwright?

## Sub-beads: TBD after research

title: M3: Persistence — SQLite mission store
description:
Crash-safe mission persistence via SQLite behind Tauri commands.

## Deliverable
- SQLite database with WAL mode via Tauri SQL plugin
- MissionStore API exposed via Tauri commands (frontend never touches DB directly)
- Schema versioning with migrations
- Autosave (default 60s, configurable)
- Backup: single mirror copy with atomic write (temp+rename)
- Crash recovery: detect paused mission on restart, prompt user
- Archive: ZIP export of completed missions

## Schema (from plugin investigation — cite: layers/schema.py v4)

### missions
id, name, status (idle/active/paused/finished/finalized), start_time, pause_time, finish_time, paused_seconds, notes, schema_version

### devices
id, mission_id, device_id, name, color, last_seen, status (online/offline/unknown)

### positions
id, mission_id, device_id, name, lat, lon, altitude, speed, battery, accuracy, source, timestamp, data_origin (live/cache)

### markers (4 types, common + type-specific fields)
**Common:** id, mission_id, type, name, description, lat, lon, irish_grid_e, irish_grid_n, created_at, updated_at, display_order
**IPP/LKP:** subject_category
**Clue:** clue_type, confidence, found_by
**Hazard:** hazard_type, severity
**Casualty:** condition, treatment, evacuation_priority, found_by

### drawings (6 types)
**Lines:** id, mission_id, name, description, color, width, distance_m, temporary_measure, display_order
**Search Areas:** id, mission_id, name, team, status, priority, area_sqkm, POA, POD, terrain, search_method, color, start_time, end_time, notes, display_order
**Range Rings:** id, mission_id, name, center_lat, center_lon, radius_m, label, color, lpb_category, percentile, display_order
**Bearing Lines:** id, mission_id, name, origin_lat, origin_lon, bearing, distance_m, label, color, display_order
**Search Sectors:** id, mission_id, name, center_lat, center_lon, start_bearing, end_bearing, radius_m, arc_length_deg, area_sqkm, priority, color, display_order
**Text Labels:** id, mission_id, text, lat, lon, font_size, color, rotation, display_order

### mission_events
id, mission_id, event_type, timestamp, details_json

## Persistence Rules (from investigation)
- Feature edits (markers, drawings, positions): saved immediately
- Mission state transitions: saved immediately
- Project-level save (GeoPackage integrity check + backup sync): on autosave timer
- Backup: single mirror, atomic write via temp+rename, no rotation
- Archive: ZIP with GeoPackage snapshot + attachments, validated after creation

## Dependencies
- M1 complete (Tauri running)

## Spike reference
- spikes/S5-persistence/ — MissionStore prototype, schema, migrations
- Investigation output: /tmp/m3-persistence.md

title: S2: Irish Grid Accuracy — proj4js TM65/ITM validation
description:
Spike to validate that proj4js can replace QgsCoordinateTransform for Irish Grid coordinate conversion with acceptable accuracy.

## Goal
Prove TM65 ↔ WGS84 ↔ ITM conversion accuracy matches QGIS output within acceptable tolerances for SAR operations.

## Tasks
- [ ] Read and understand coordinates.py in the current plugin
- [ ] Build a golden dataset: 20+ known Kerry reference points from QGIS
- [ ] Test proj4js TOWGS84 7-param transform against golden dataset
- [ ] Measure max error — is it < 5m? < 1m?
- [ ] If not acceptable, investigate NTv2 grid shift approach
- [ ] Test Irish Grid reference formatting (letter + 6-digit format)
- [ ] Test bearing calculations (true + magnetic) against QGIS output

## BLOCKING
If accuracy is not acceptable, we need a different coordinate approach before building anything else.

## Reference
spikes/S2-irish-grid/README.md
~/Documents/Qgis/sartracker/utils/coordinates.py

title: M1: Scaffold — complete project setup
description:
Single bead: set up the entire project foundation in one session.

## Steps (in order)
1. `create-tauri-app` with Vite + React + TypeScript template
2. Verify `npm run tauri dev` opens a window
3. Install all core dependencies:
   - maplibre-gl, @turf/turf, proj4, terra-draw
   - zustand
   - @tauri-apps/plugin-sql
   - tailwindcss + postcss + autoprefixer
4. Configure Tailwind (postcss.config, tailwind.config)
5. TypeScript strict mode in tsconfig
6. ESLint configured
7. Create src/ folder structure: lib/, components/, hooks/, utils/, types/
8. Create tests/ folder structure: unit/, e2e/, fixtures/
9. Install and configure vitest
10. Install and configure Playwright
11. Add npm scripts: dev, build, test, test:e2e, test:all
12. Write one vitest smoke test (assert true)
13. Write one Playwright smoke test (app opens, has content)
14. .gitignore (node_modules, dist, target, .tauri)

## Acceptance criteria
- `npm run tauri dev` opens a desktop window with React content
- `npm run test` passes (vitest)
- `npm run test:e2e` passes (Playwright)
- TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind classes work
- All MapLibre/proj4/Zustand imports resolve
- Folder structure matches CLAUDE.md spec

## Decisions (locked)
- Tauri 2.0 stable
- Raw MapLibre via useRef (no react-map-gl)
- Zustand for state
- Tailwind CSS
- No router
- Tauri SQL plugin

Replaces: sartracker-web-2ps, sartracker-web-593, sartracker-web-mu1

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Implemented the scaffold in-repo: Vite + React + TypeScript, src-tauri initialized, Tailwind/ESLint/Vitest/Playwright configured, core dependencies installed, Zustand-backed app shell added, and smoke tests passing via npm run test, lint, build, test:e2e, and test:all. npm run tauri dev starts Vite and native compilation successfully; I stopped it after confirming startup to avoid leaving long-running watchers active.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Final verification after metadata cleanup: cargo check passed for src-tauri, the Rust crate and bundle identifiers were renamed to project-specific values, and the scaffold now meets the bead deliverables with green test, lint, build, and Playwright smoke coverage.

title: M4: Tracking — Traccar HTTP polling + map rendering
description:
Real-time GPS tracking from Traccar server displayed on the map.

## Deliverable
- TraccarClient: authenticate, fetch devices, fetch positions, fetch breadcrumbs
- PollingManager: configurable interval (30s default), retry with exponential backoff
- Device markers on map (coloured circles per device)
- Breadcrumb trails per device (dotted lines, same colour as device)
- Stale device detection + visual indicator
- Last-good cache with offline mode warning
- Incremental breadcrumb fetching (per-device timestamp tracking)
- Positions persisted to SQLite via MissionStore

## Decisions (locked)
- HTTP polling for v1 (30s default, configurable)
- Mock auth for tests; real credentials from Eamonn when available
- Device colours: MD5 hash of device_id, deterministic, 50-255 range, collision-resistant
- Breadcrumb trails: dotted lines (width 1.5), current positions: circle markers (size 5)
- Stale device: 1hr visibility threshold, 5min cache TTL, yellow indicator
- Last-good cache: full per-device snapshots + breadcrumbs, JSON on disk, 4hr max age, atomic writes
- Traccar unreachable: serve cached data, OFFLINE MODE warning, never clear map, CONNECTION RESTORED on recovery
- Incremental breadcrumbs: per-device timestamp tracking, dedup by (device_id, timestamp), 100K max, FIFO eviction
- JSON cache is transport-only; SQLite (MissionStore) is always authoritative
- Canonical test fixtures to be stored in tests/fixtures/

## Traccar API Payloads (from plugin investigation)

### Device response (/api/devices)
Required: id (int)
Optional: name (fallback 'Device {id}'), status, lastUpdate, uniqueId, category

### Position response (/api/positions)
Required: deviceId (int), latitude (float), longitude (float), one of fixTime/serverTime/deviceTime
Optional: id, altitude, speed, valid, attributes.batteryLevel

### Normalisation: API → internal
deviceId → device_id (string), latitude → lat, longitude → lon, fixTime → ts (ISO8601 UTC), altitude → altitude, speed → speed, attributes.batteryLevel → battery
Post-annotation: data_origin (live/cache), cache_age_seconds, device_cache_stale

## Dependencies
- M2 complete ✅
- M3 complete ✅

## Spike reference
- spikes/S7-traccar-integration/
- Investigation: /tmp/m4-fixtures.md

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Implemented M4 tracking end-to-end. Added Traccar client, polling manager, last-good cache + Tauri cache adapter, runtime orchestration, map overlay sync, stale/offline/recovery behavior, and mission-store persistence wiring. Added canonical fixtures plus unit/integration/E2E coverage. Verification: npm run test, npm run lint, npm run build, npm run test:e2e, cargo check, cargo test all green. Remaining non-code follow-up is live validation against real Traccar credentials when available.

title: M4: Tracking — Traccar HTTP polling + map rendering
description:
Real-time GPS tracking from Traccar server displayed on the map.

## Deliverable
- TraccarClient: authenticate, fetch devices, fetch positions, fetch breadcrumbs
- PollingManager: configurable interval (30s default), retry with exponential backoff
- Device markers on map (coloured circles per device)
- Breadcrumb trails per device (dotted lines, same colour as device)
- Stale device detection + visual indicator
- Last-good cache with offline mode warning
- Incremental breadcrumb fetching (per-device timestamp tracking)
- Positions persisted to SQLite via MissionStore

## Behaviour (from plugin investigation — cite: traccar_http.py, provider_controller.py, tracking_manager.py)

### Device Colours
- MD5 hash of device_id → deterministic, persistent, collision-resistant
- Range: 50-255 per channel (avoids dark/invisible)
- Collision check: if within 30 units on all channels, shift using second hash half
- NOT user-configurable in v1

### Breadcrumb Styling
- Dotted lines (line_style: dot, width: 1.5, round join/cap)
- Current positions: circle markers (size 5)
- Trails segmented on time gaps (5 min default)

### Stale Device
- Visibility threshold: 1 hour (device hidden from active list if no update for 1hr)
- Cache TTL: 5 minutes (provider-level)
- Visual: green (online), red (offline), yellow (unknown/stale)

### Last-Good Cache
- Full per-device position snapshots + breadcrumbs
- Stored as JSON on disk, atomic writes (temp+rename+fsync)
- Max age: 4 hours
- Individual position validation (bad entries dropped, not whole cache)

### Traccar Unreachable
- Serve cached data with OFFLINE MODE warning
- NEVER clear existing map data on empty/failed response
- Track consecutive failures + outage duration
- Show CONNECTION RESTORED notification when back

### Incremental Breadcrumbs
- Per-device latest-timestamp tracking via BreadcrumbAccumulator
- Only fetch positions newer than last known per device
- Deduplication by (device_id, timestamp)
- Max 100K positions in memory, FIFO eviction
- Reset on new mission, preserved on pause/resume

## Decisions (locked)
- HTTP polling for v1 (decided during spike research)
- Polling interval: 30s default, configurable
- Mock auth for tests; real credentials from Eamonn when available

## Dependencies
- M2 complete (map must be rendering)
- M3 complete (persistence for storing positions)

## Spike reference
- spikes/S7-traccar-integration/ — TraccarClient, PollingManager
- Investigation output: /tmp/m4-tracking.md

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Readiness review: Research required = Medium. Implementation readiness = 4/5. Recommended action: implement after a short design pass. Locked enough to start: HTTP polling for v1, 30s default polling, deterministic device colours, incremental breadcrumb fetching, stale/offline semantics, last-good cache requirement, SQLite persistence via MissionStore. Open questions needing confirmation/research: 1) exact Traccar auth/session approach for production (basic auth vs token vs cookie/session handling, SSL expectations, failure modes), 2) exact operator-facing offline/degraded UI location and wording, 3) paused mission behavior boundary: bead says tracking data still accepted while auto-refresh is suppressed and manual refresh works, so we should confirm whether background polling fully stops during pause or continues but is hidden/suppressed in UI, 4) exact cache file ownership/location/retention policy if we want parity with the plugin. Main architectural note: keep Traccar client, polling/backoff, cache, persistence write path, and map layer shaping as separate boundaries; do not bury tracking orchestration inside React components.

title: M5: Mission UI — start/pause/resume/finish + timers
description:
Mission lifecycle controls and dual timer display.

## Deliverable
- Mission control panel: Start, Pause/Resume, Finish buttons (colour-coded, flashing when paused)
- Dual timers: Elapsed (total since start) + Active Search (excludes pauses)
- Mission name input on start
- Start offset: back-date mission start 0-48 hours
- Finish confirmation dialog — data remains editable after finish
- Auto-detect paused mission on restart → prompt Resume or Start Fresh
- Mission state persisted to SQLite

## NOT in M5 scope (deferred to Phase 3)
- Finalize (archive + lock read-only)
- Admin unlock
- Focus mode

## Lifecycle Model (canonical — resolves all contradictions)

### Persisted statuses in SQLite
`active`, `paused`, `finished`, `finalized` — these are real database values in the missions table.
`idle` is NOT a persisted status — it means 'no active mission' and is represented by the absence of an active/paused record.

### State machine (UI layer)
IDLE → ACTIVE → PAUSED ↔ ACTIVE → FINISHED
- IDLE is the app-shell state when no mission is active
- FINISHED is a terminal persisted state (unlike the plugin where it was transient)
- The mission record stays `finished` in the database until explicitly finalized (Phase 3)
- The UI resets to idle-like behaviour after finish (start button enabled, timers stopped)

### Valid transitions
- IDLE → ACTIVE (start_mission)
- ACTIVE → PAUSED (pause_mission)
- PAUSED → ACTIVE (resume_mission)
- ACTIVE → FINISHED (finish_mission)
- PAUSED → FINISHED (finish_mission while paused)
- No backwards from FINISHED. Must start a new mission.

## Pause Behaviour
- Active search timer **stops**. Elapsed timer **keeps running**.
- Tracking polling timer keeps ticking but **suppresses the actual refresh call**. No new data fetched.
- No background data accumulation while paused.
- Markers and drawings CAN still be placed/edited.
- Mission is still considered 'running' for purposes like breadcrumb filtering.
- UI: Pause button becomes Resume with flashing animation.

## Finish Behaviour
- Stops all timing.
- Mission record in SQLite set to `finished`.
- UI resets: Start enabled, Pause/Finish disabled.
- Data remains **editable** — not read-only.
- Tracking auto-refresh stops. Manual review of existing data still possible.
- Saved pause state cleared.
- Breadcrumb accumulator preserved until next start_mission.

## Start Fresh (on restart with paused mission)
- Dismisses the paused mission prompt.
- Clears the saved pause state from SQLite.
- Does NOT delete or archive the old mission data — it remains on disk.
- User must then start a new mission manually.
- If user enters the same mission name as an existing mission, warn (don't silently overwrite).

## Back-dated Start
- Range: 0-48 hours via spinner/input.
- Start time validated: must be timezone-aware UTC, must not be in the future.
- Breadcrumbs fetched from the back-dated time.
- Use case: teams already moving before command post starts, or replaying test data.

## Single Active Mission
- One active mission at a time.
- Starting new mission while paused is blocked — must resume or finish first.
- Multiple finished missions can coexist on disk.

## Timer Formulas (exact)
```
elapsed = max(0, now - mission_start_ts)
```
Where mission_start_ts respects the back-dated offset.

```
total_paused = sum of all completed pause durations + current ongoing pause (if any)
active = max(0, elapsed - total_paused)
```

On each resume: `paused_total += (now - pause_started_at)`
On finish while paused: same accumulation before stopping.

### Example
| Event | Time | paused_total | active |
|---|---|---|---|
| Start | T+0 | 0 | 0 |
| Pause | T+300 | 0 | 300 |
| Resume | T+600 | 300 | 300 |
| Pause | T+1200 | 300 | 900 |
| Resume | T+1500 | 600 | 900 |
| Finish | T+2000 | 600 | 1400 |

## Recovery on Restart
1. On app startup: query SQLite for mission with status = `active` or `paused`
2. If found with `paused`: show Resume/Start Fresh dialog
3. If found with `active` (crash during active mission): treat as paused, show same dialog
4. Resume: restore mission context, timers recalculated from persisted timestamps
5. Start Fresh: clear the mission's pause state, leave data on disk, return to idle

## Operator Dialog Copy (from plugin)

### Resume/Start Fresh prompt
Title: 'Resume Mission?'
Body: 'Found paused mission:\n\nMission: {name}\nStarted: {start_time}\n\nDo you want to resume this mission?'
Buttons: 'Resume Mission' (default) | 'Start Fresh'

### Finish confirmation
Title: 'End Mission'
Body: 'Are you sure you want to end this mission?\n\nThis will:\n• Stop mission timers\n• Keep all mission data editable\n• Reset UI for the next mission\n\nMission data remains saved.\nUse Finalize Mission later to archive and lock the data.'
Buttons: 'Yes' | 'No' (default No)

## Architecture
- Mission state machine lives in a dedicated domain module (not in UI components or persistence adapters)
- UI components subscribe to state changes via Zustand store
- Persistence writes happen through MissionStore Tauri commands
- Timer logic is pure functions, testable without UI

## Dependencies
- M2 complete ✅
- M3 complete ✅

## Evidence
- Plugin investigation: /tmp/m5-lifecycle.md, /tmp/m5-deep.md
- Plugin source: mission_controller.py, mission_lifecycle_controller.py, sar_panel.py

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Implemented M5 mission UI end-to-end. Added mission runtime/store, pure timer calculations, mission control panel, recovery prompt, finish confirmation, back-dated starts, duplicate-name warnings, and tracking refresh suppression while paused. Extended persistence for explicit start_time, active-mission recovery, and paused_seconds accumulation on resume/finish. Verification: npm run test, npm run lint, npm run build, npm run test:e2e, cargo check, cargo test all green. Important implementation note: Start Fresh currently resolves the recoverable mission by finishing it, which preserves data and returns the UI to idle.

comments.author: Donal
comments.text: Validation hardening follow-up completed. Added a browser-only mission harness for headless Playwright validation, serious workflow coverage for start/pause/resume/finish/recovery/invalid-offset cases, and fixed a real UI timer staleness bug after mission transitions. Also hardened tracking runtime so idle and paused polling states are distinct and malformed tracking cache payloads no longer abort startup. Verification: npm run test, npm run lint, npm run build, npm run test:e2e, cargo check, cargo test all green.

title: M2: Map — MapLibre + basemaps + coordinate display
description:
Map rendering with basemap switching and live coordinate bar.

- MapLibre GL JS rendering in the Tauri window via raw useRef (decided in M1)
- Basemap switcher: OpenTopoMap (default), ESRI Topo, OSM, Satellite
- Selected basemap persisted to localStorage
- Coordinate bar: WGS84 + Irish Grid (TM65) updating on mouse move
- Map centered on Kerry (51.97, -9.70), zoom 12
- Tile caching via Service Worker for offline resilience

## Decisions (locked)
- Raw MapLibre via useRef — no react-map-gl (decided M1 research)
- proj4js for TM65 conversion with TOWGS84 params from S2 spike
- Service Worker for tile caching (Tauri filesystem for GeoPackage later)

title: M6: Markers — IPP/LKP, clues, hazards, casualties
description:
Click-to-place markers with type-specific forms and persistence.

## Deliverable
- Click map to place marker
- Type selector: IPP/LKP, Clue, Hazard, Casualty
- Modal dialog with type-specific fields
- Auto-populated coordinates (WGS84 + ITM + TM65 grid ref)
- Markers persisted to SQLite via MissionStore
- Edit and delete markers
- Labels always visible on map

## Decisions (locked — from plugin investigation)

### Marker Icons
| Type | Shape | Colour | Size |
|------|-------|--------|------|
| IPP/LKP | Star | #0066FF (blue) | 7 |
| Clue | Circle | #FFFFFF (white fill) | 10 |
| Hazard | Inverted arrowhead | #FF0000 (red) | 12 |
| Casualty | Star | #FF0000 (red) | 16, with drop shadow |

Implement as custom SVGs matching these shapes/colours.

### Form UI
- Modal dialog (not sidebar panel)
- Layout: type selector (radio buttons) → coordinates (read-only) → type-specific fields → Cancel/Save buttons
- Type-specific fields shown/hidden dynamically based on selection
- Min width ~500px

### Label Display
- Always visible (no hover, no zoom threshold, no toggle)
- Field: name
- Placement: over point
- Font: 10pt, colour matches marker type
- White halo/buffer for readability

### Label Colours
- IPP/LKP: #0066FF (blue)
- Clue: #806600 (dark gold)
- Hazard: #8B0000 (dark red)
- Casualty: #8B0000 (dark red)

### Subject Categories (IPP/LKP — fixed dropdown)
Child (1-3 years), Child (4-6 years), Child (7-12 years), Hiker, Hunter, Elderly, Dementia Patient, Despondent, Autistic, Other

### Clue Types (fixed dropdown)
Footprint, Clothing, Equipment, Witness Sighting, Physical Evidence, Other

### Confidence Levels (fixed dropdown)
Confirmed, Probable, Possible

### Hazard Types (fixed dropdown)
Cliff/Drop-off, Water Hazard, Bog/Peatland, Dense Vegetation, Wildlife Danger, Weather Exposure, Other

### Hazard Severity (fixed dropdown, default Medium)
Critical, High, Medium, Low

### Casualty Conditions (fixed dropdown)
Injured - Conscious, Injured - Unconscious, Deceased, Unresponsive, Medical Emergency, Unknown

### Evacuation Priority (fixed dropdown)
Immediate, Urgent, Delayed, None Required

### Fields Per Type
**Common:** name, description, lat, lon, irish_grid_e, irish_grid_n, created_at, updated_at, display_order
**IPP/LKP:** + subject_category
**Clue:** + clue_type, confidence, found_by
**Hazard:** + hazard_type, severity
**Casualty:** + condition, treatment, evacuation_priority, found_by

## Dependencies
- M2 complete ✅
- M3 complete ✅

## Spike reference
- spikes/S3-drawing-tools/ — marker placement prototype
- Investigation: /tmp/m6-markers.md
